known_predecessor_sink.meaning: The selector 0xC8 body writes or builds the authCode/OTL continuation string into child +0x38 before entering this postwrite continuation.

state_transition.meaning: Immediately after the authCode/OTL continuation material is prepared, the controller child state field is set to 0x14.

gate.meaning: Low-bit flag must be set to take the dispatch path.
A local pointer must also be present; otherwise execution skips to cleanup flag handling.

table_walk.meaning: Selects an entry through manager ranges at +0x100/+0x108 or fallback +0x198.
Loads a virtual/slot callback from the selected entry's vtable or method table.
Selects an R8D count/value from manager ranges at +0x1A0/+0x1A8 or fallback +0x1F8.
Invokes the selected callback with RCX = selected entry and R8D = selected count/value.

optional_global_dispatch.bounded_inference: This looks like a post-authCode notification or manager callback, not a socket packet encode path. The code selects an entry from a global manager and calls slot +0x20 when both local gates pass.

cleanup_paths.condition: low local flag byte equals 6 or 7
sequence: Calls 0x140638A90 with RCX/RDX = RBX and R8 from [RBX] +0x8.
Calls 0x1407B8478 twice with EDX = 0x58 and EDX = 0x10.
cleanup_paths.bounded_inference: This is a cleanup or release path for a local temporary object rooted at [RBP-0x31].

cleanup_paths.condition: low local flag byte equals 4 and bit 0 of the high shifted flag byte is set
sequence: Calls 0x1407C4634 with RCX = [RBP-0x31] when the bit is set.
cleanup_paths.bounded_inference: This is a second conditional cleanup path for the same local temporary object family.

cleanup_paths.condition: shared tail local pointer [RBP-0x21] is non-null
cleanup_paths.bounded_inference: The shared tail releases or finalizes a separate temporary string/object.

negative_evidence: The postwrite continuation has no string literal references in 0x14029E970..0x14029EA4C.
The only seeded controller-child field event in this window is the child +0x0 state write to 0x14.
This window does not touch request/event +0x80/+0x88 or +0xD0 directly; those sources were consumed in the earlier selector bodies.

confirmed: Selector 0xC8 does not stop at the child +0x38 authCode/OTL sink; it immediately transitions the controller child state to 0x14.
After state 0x14, the code may dispatch through a process-global manager rooted at 0x140E57B40 and method slot +0x20.
The remainder of the window is dominated by local temporary cleanup paths and the shared tail cleanup.
reversing_result.bounded_inference: State 0x14 is the post-authCode/OTL continuation state. The global-manager callback likely notifies or schedules downstream handling after the authCode material is staged, while the later branches clean up temporary string/object state.
reversing_result.opennxt_relevance: For OpenNXT 947, selector 0xC8 should be modeled as a two-step native contract: request/event +0x80/+0x88 supplies the authCode/OTL string into child +0x38, then the client moves to state 0x14 and may schedule internal follow-up work. This remains separate from selector 0x12E route opening and selector 0x1A6 social-code error handling.

next_targets: Trace consumers of controller child state 0x14 to identify the downstream follow-up work scheduled after authCode staging.
Trace references to global manager 0x140E57B40 around this call shape to name the callback family.
Classify direct cleanup helpers 0x140638A90, 0x1407B8478, 0x1407C4634, and 0x1406330C0 so temporary object lifetimes can be named rather than inferred.
